headline: AES Corp.: Exiting The Stock Exchange
summary: AES Corporation is being acquired by a BlackRock/EQT-led consortium for $33.4B. Learn more about the deal and my recommendation for AES stock.

headline: BlackRock Reshapes Power Exposure With AES Buyout And Naturgy Exit
summary: BlackRock, through its Global Infrastructure Partners division, is leading a consortium to acquire AES Corporation in one of the larger recent infrastructure buyouts. The deal centers on growing electricity demand linked to data center expansion and AI related power needs. Separately, BlackRock has fully exited its position in Spanish energy company Naturgy, closing out its prior stake. These moves highlight a reshaping of BlackRock’s energy and infrastructure exposure that could influence...

headline: Seaport Research Upgrades The AES Corporation (AES) to Neutral
summary: The AES Corporation (NYSE:AES) is among the 13 Most Undervalued S&P 500 Stocks to Invest In. On February 27, 2026, Seaport Research analyst Angie Storozynski upgraded The AES Corporation (NYSE:AES) to Neutral from Sell. On February 20, 2026, Morgan Stanley lowered its price target on AES Corp. to $23 from $24 and maintained an Overweight […]

headline: Is AES (AES) Still Attractive After A 38% Gain And Recent Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether AES at around US$14.18 is still offering good value or if the easy gains are behind it, you are not alone. The stock is up 38.5% over the past year, even though the last 7 days showed a 12.8% decline and the year to date return sits at a 4.3% decline, which can change how investors think about both upside and risk. Recent news around AES has focused on broader coverage of the company and its positioning within the utilities sector. This helps frame those price...
